index.name: Nifty50 Value 20 Index
index.description: Nifty50 Value 20 is a smart-beta 'factor index' that selects the 20 stocks from Nifty 50 with the highest composite 'value score' — a proprietary NSE formula weighting Return on Capital Employed (ROCE), Price-to-Earnings (PE), Price-to-Book (PB), and Dividend Yield. The underlying philosophy is Benjamin Graham's Value Investing: buy fundamentally strong companies at cheap prices and let mean reversion deliver alpha. In India's growth-focused market, value investing has historically struggled to consistently outperform quality/growth strategies because Indian markets reward high-growth companies with persistent premium multiples. However, value ETFs like NV20BEES shine during market corrections and rate-rising cycles — when expensive growth stocks get de-rated but cheap industrials, PSU banks and commodity stocks hold or outperform. The 20-stock construct means very high concentration risk — a single stock can be 8-12% of the index, amplifying sector-specific or stock-specific impact. NV20BEES typically holds PSU banks, ITC, coal/oil PSUs, and value-screened Nifty 50 cyclicals that trade below historical P/B or P/E. It's a tactical/satellite allocation tool rather than a core portfolio holding — investors use it as a 10-15% value tilt overlay on a main Nifty 50 or multi-cap holding. Nippon India AMC is one of India's oldest mutual fund houses with strong ETF product management capabilities.

holdings.name: ITC Ltd
holdings.description: ITC is the quintessential Indian value stock — cigarettes generate Rs 16,000 Cr+ EBIT at 65%+ margins, funding FMCG brand building across Aashirvaad, Sunfeast, Bingo, Classmate. Hotels demerger unlocking value. Trading at 25x PE vs global FMCG peers at 35-40x. Highest dividend yield among Nifty 50 blue chips at 3%+. ROCE above 25% consistently.

holdings.name: Coal India
holdings.description: World's largest coal mining company producing 700+ million tonnes annually. India's baseload power dominance ensures coal demand for 15+ years. Dividend yield 5%+ with government mandated payouts. ROCE above 50% on minimal reinvestment capex. Transition risk is a 15-year horizon concern, not immediate.

holdings.name: ONGC
holdings.description: India's largest oil and gas explorer with 80%+ domestic crude reserves ownership. KG-DWN-98/2 deepwater project adding 40,000+ bopd. Perpetually undervalued on market fears of government dividend extraction. P/B ratio below 1x makes it a classic Graham-style value play. ONGC Videsh provides international E&P diversification.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corporation
holdings.description: India's monopoly inter-state power transmission PSU. 1.7L+ circuit km of transmission lines. Regulated return on equity (15.5%) business with near-zero earnings risk. Dividend yield 4-5% with inflation-linked tariff escalation. RE (renewable energy) push adding green energy corridors doubling capex opportunities.

holdings.name: NTPC Ltd
holdings.description: India's largest power generator with 70GW+ installed capacity. Regulated tariff model guarantees return on equity. Green energy subsidiary NTPC Renewable targeting 60GW by 2032. Consistently trades below replacement cost. Dividend payout 45%+ of profits. NTPC Hydro provides additional regulated return streams.

holdings.name: Vedanta Ltd
holdings.description: India's largest diversified natural resources company — zinc (Hindustan Zinc), aluminium (Balco, Jharsuguda), iron ore, oil (Cairn India), copper and silver. Dividend yield has touched 15-20% in value cycles. India's only primary zinc producer with 75% global zinc from BALAMA project. Anil Agarwal's Vedanta Resources parent leverage is key risk to monitor.

holdings.name: SBI (State Bank of India)
holdings.description: India's largest bank — Rs 60 lakh crore balance sheet, 500M+ customers, 22,000+ branches. P/B ratio of 1.2-1.5x vs private bank peers at 2.5-4x despite comparable ROE (15%+). SBI Cards, SBI Life, SBI MF subsidiaries unlocking value. Agricultural and Mudra loans serve India's grassroots economy with priority sector coverage.

holdings.name: Hindustan Petroleum (HPCL)
holdings.description: India's second-largest public sector oil marketing company. 9,000+ petrol stations with retail fuel marketing advantage. Vizag and Mumbai refineries being upgraded for complex crude processing. Trades at significant discount to replacement cost. Earnings highly cyclical with marketing margins — value opportunity deepens in refining downcycles.

holdings.name: BPCL (Bharat Petroleum)
holdings.description: Third-largest PSU oil company with Kochi, Mumbai and Bina refineries. Privatisation narrative (repeatedly delayed) creates periodic re-rating events. Gas, LNG and EV charging diversification reducing pure refining dependency. Free cash flow generation robust in positive marketing margin environments. Dividend yield attractive at 4%+.

holdings.name: GAIL India
holdings.description: India's largest natural gas transmission and distribution company. 16,000+ km pipeline network — monopoly trunk transmission. LNG regasification capacity expanding at Dabhol and Mundra. City Gas Distribution investments (MGL, IGL stakes) provide urban upside. Petchem segment (Petronet Pata complex) improving utilisation. Trading at 10x PE with 3% dividend yield.

index.description: Kotak Mahindra Mutual Fund - Kotak Nifty200 Quality 30 ETF tracks the Nifty200 Quality 30 Index index.

holdings.name: TCS
holdings.description: India's quality flagship. 40%+ EBITDA margin, 55%+ ROCE, near-zero debt. Consistent dividend payer with buyback track record. Quality factor anchor.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Second-largest IT quality blue chip. 30%+ EBITDA margins, 35%+ ROCE. Consistent earnings and dividend. GenAI via Infosys Topaz platform.

holdings.name: HUL
holdings.description: Consumer quality benchmark. 50%+ ROCE, 20%+ net margin, 5%+ dividend yield. Low earnings variability across economic cycles.

holdings.name: Nestle India
holdings.description: Maggi monopoly with zero long-term debt. 60%+ ROCE. Premium portfolio of Munch, KitKat, Nescafe. Consistent quality scoring.

holdings.name: Asian Paints
holdings.description: Duopoly paints market leader. 60%+ gross margins, asset-light distribution. B2C brand moat. Quality perennial inclusion.

holdings.name: Bajaj Finance
holdings.description: India's highest-quality NBFC. 20%+ ROE, 400M+ customer data asset. Consumer lending with proprietary credit models.

holdings.name: Titan Company
holdings.description: Watches, jewellery, eyewear — premium aspirational consumer. 25%+ ROCE, consistent cash generation. Tata Group quality governance.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Quality banking franchise. 16-18% ROE, 1.1-1.2% RoA, lowest NPA among peers. Consistent dividend. Largest private bank by market cap.

holdings.name: Wipro
holdings.description: IT services with consistent quality metrics. Capital allocation discipline — recurring buybacks. Diversified BFSI, healthcare and energy verticals.

holdings.name: Pidilite Industries
holdings.description: Fevicol monopoly in wood adhesives. Consumer and construction segments. 35%+ ROCE with low debt. Massive distribution network.

name: Mirae Asset Mutual Fund - Mirae Asset BSE 500 Dividend Leaders 50 ETF

index.name: BSE 500 Dividend Leaders
index.description: Mirae Asset Mutual Fund - Mirae Asset BSE 500 Dividend Leaders 50 ETF tracks the BSE 500 Dividend Leaders index.

holdings.name: Coal India
holdings.description: Highest dividend yielder in Nifty 500 at 5-8% annually. Government mandated dividend payouts — 50%+ of profits distributed. Minimal capex requirement.

holdings.name: ONGC
holdings.description: Consistent 4-5% dividend yield. Government-owned oil company with mandatory dividend policy. ONGC Videsh cash flows supporting dividend capacity.

holdings.name: IOC
holdings.description: India's largest oil company by revenue. 4-5% dividend yield with consistent payout. Dividend Opportunities 50 inclusion from high trailing yield.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corporation
holdings.description: Regulated utility mandated to distribute dividends. 4-5% yield. Inflation-indexed tariff escalation ensures growing dividend capacity.

holdings.name: ITC Ltd
holdings.description: Highest dividend yield among Nifty 50 FMCG at 3%+. Cigarettes generative Rs 16,000 Cr+ EBIT funding generous dividend payouts.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Consistent 2%+ dividend yield plus special dividends. Buyback programme supplementing regular payout. IT sector's most consistent dividend track record.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: 45%+ profit distributed as dividend. Government holds 51% requiring consistent payouts. Regulated ROE ensures dividend sustainability.

holdings.name: GAIL India
holdings.description: 3% dividend yield at current market price. Government-owned gas transmission monopoly with stable cash flows.

holdings.name: HPCL
holdings.description: High but volatile dividend yield (3-6%) tied to marketing margins. Deep value dividend play when crude prices stabilise.

holdings.name: SBI
holdings.description: Growing dividend from rising profits. Government holds 57% requiring mandatory annual dividends. PSU bank profitability normalising post-NPA cycle.
